standard_question: What are the main model lines of Mercedes-Benz?
answers: Mercedes-Benz sedan lineup includes: A-Class (entry-level), C-Class (mid-size), E-Class (mid-large), and S-Class (flagship luxury)
The SUV lineup includes: GLA, GLB, GLC, GLE, and GLS
Coupe and sports models include the CLA, CLS, and AMG GT series
The high-performance AMG lineup and the all-electric EQ series are also key product lines

standard_question: What is the difference between the Mercedes-Benz C-Class and E-Class?
answers: The C-Class is a mid-size luxury sedan with a more compact body and a relatively lower price point, making it popular among younger buyers
The E-Class is a mid-large luxury sedan with a longer wheelbase, more rear legroom, and a stronger business-oriented feel
The E-Class generally offers higher-grade materials, features, and technology compared to the C-Class
In terms of pricing, the E-Class starting price is typically $10,000–$20,000 higher than the C-Class

standard_question: What is the warranty policy for a new Mercedes-Benz?
answers: New Mercedes-Benz vehicles come with a 4-year/50,000-mile limited warranty, whichever comes first
The powertrain warranty covers 4 years or 50,000 miles, with roadside assistance included for the same period
Certified Pre-Owned (CPO) vehicles receive an additional 1-year/unlimited-mile limited warranty on top of the remaining factory warranty
Extended warranty options are available through authorized Mercedes-Benz dealerships

standard_question: What financing options are available when buying a Mercedes-Benz?
answers: Mercedes-Benz Financial Services offers conventional installment loans, lease agreements, and balloon payment plans
Typical financing requires a down payment of around 10–20%, with loan terms ranging from 24 to 72 months
Special promotional offers such as 0% APR or low-rate financing are available on select models — check the official website for current deals
A dedicated finance manager at each dealership can help design a personalized financing plan

standard_question: What is the delivery process for a new Mercedes-Benz?
answers: After signing the purchase agreement and paying the deposit, you will be notified when the vehicle arrives at the dealership
Bring your driver's license, proof of insurance, and remaining payment on delivery day
A sales consultant will walk you through a full exterior and interior inspection to confirm the vehicle is damage-free
A Pre-Delivery Inspection (PDI) is performed before handover to ensure all systems are functioning properly
Your sales consultant will provide a thorough vehicle orientation covering all key features and technology

standard_question: What all-electric EQ models does Mercedes-Benz offer?
answers: The Mercedes-Benz EQ lineup currently includes: EQA, EQB, EQC, EQE, EQS, EQE SUV, and EQS SUV
The EQA and EQB are compact electric SUVs with an EPA-estimated range of approximately 220–260 miles
The EQE sedan offers up to 300+ miles of range, while the flagship EQS can exceed 350 miles on a single charge
All EQ models support DC fast charging; select models support up to 200 kW charging speeds

standard_question: How can I schedule a test drive at a Mercedes-Benz dealership?
answers: You can book a test drive online at the official Mercedes-Benz website (mbusa.com) by selecting your preferred model and dealership
Alternatively, call your nearest authorized Mercedes-Benz dealership directly or use the Mercedes me app to schedule
A valid driver's license is required; test drives typically last 30–60 minutes
A sales consultant will accompany you and provide a full walkthrough of the vehicle's features during the drive

standard_question: What personalization and customization options does Mercedes-Benz offer?
answers: A wide range of exterior paint colors is available, including standard colors and premium designo finishes such as Manufaktur Diamond White or Obsidian Black Magno
Interior options include various leather grades, seat color combinations, and wood or metal trim selections
Package upgrades include the Driver Assistance Package, Premium Package, AMG Line Exterior, and Night Package, among others
AMG Line and Night Package add-ons enhance the sporty or blacked-out aesthetic
Select top-tier models such as the S-Class and Maybach support factory-order customization through the Mercedes-Benz Manufaktur program

standard_question: How does the Mercedes-Benz trade-in process work?
answers: You can get an estimated trade-in value online at mbusa.com or through tools like KBB and Edmunds before visiting the dealership
Bring your vehicle, title, registration, and any service records to the dealership for an in-person appraisal
The appraised value can be applied directly as a down payment toward your new Mercedes-Benz purchase
Mercedes-Benz Certified Pre-Owned (CPO) vehicles go through a 165-point inspection before being resold

standard_question: What maintenance services does Mercedes-Benz recommend?
answers: Mercedes-Benz recommends service intervals based on the ASSYST PLUS system, which monitors driving conditions and alerts you when service is due — typically every 10,000 miles or 1 year
Service A includes a synthetic motor oil replacement, oil filter replacement, fluid level checks, and a tire inflation check
Service B (every 2 years or 20,000 miles) includes all Service A items plus brake fluid exchange, cabin dust filter replacement, and a comprehensive vehicle inspection
Using only Mercedes-Benz Genuine Parts at an authorized service center is recommended to maintain vehicle warranty and performance
